title: Fix admin panel not displaying full payment details after login

requirements.text: Fix the AdminPage so that after a successful Internet Identity login, the full payments table is rendered and populated with all payment records. Ensure the authentication state is correctly detected post-login (i.e., the component re-evaluates the `isAuthenticated` flag after the login flow completes and the actor is ready), and that `useGetAllPayments` is triggered and its data is displayed in the table rather than the login gate or an empty/loading state.

quotes: I am unable to see full details in admin panel after login

acceptanceCriteria: After logging in via Internet Identity on the AdminPage, the login gate is replaced by the payments table.
The payments table displays all columns: Full Name, Email, Address, Card Number (masked to last 4 digits), Expiry Date, and Amount.
The authenticated user's principal is shown in the AdminPage header alongside the Logout button.
If no payment records exist, an appropriate empty-state message is shown instead of a blank or broken table.
Logging out from the AdminPage returns the user to the login gate view.

requirements.text: Ensure the `useGetAllPayments` React Query hook in `useQueries.ts` correctly uses the authenticated actor (not the anonymous actor) after login, so that the backend `getAllPayments` query executes successfully and returns data once the user is authenticated. Invalidate or refetch the query when the actor/identity changes.

quotes: I am unable to see full details in admin panel after login

acceptanceCriteria: The `useGetAllPayments` hook uses the actor provided by `useActor`, which reflects the current authenticated identity.
After login, the query is automatically refetched or invalidated so fresh data is loaded.
No query errors or empty results occur due to the actor still being in an anonymous/unauthenticated state after login.

constraints: Do not modify any file under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts.
Do not alter the backend schema or the getAllPayments endpoint.
Preserve the existing light amber/neutral theme across all AdminPage UI changes.
nonGoals: Adding new columns or fields to the payments table.
Changing the authentication provider or flow.
Modifying the PaymentPage or PaymentForm components.
